content:
Good morning folks. I appreciate your patience. I forgot to bring water with me. As a reminder, if you're
following me on Twitter, please give a 5x5 if the volume is okay. I usually see people coming to Twitter to tell
me the volume is really low. I don't know what to do to make it louder except for tell you to turn your device
up. Everything I have over here is set to the highest volume.

Throughout this week, I've mentioned that I like the 4104 level, specifically 4104.75, as an upside draw if
price got animated to the upside. So far, price has come down into our new day opening Gap. This gap is
formed between the close at 5 o'clock and the open at 6 o'clock. You want to have this on your charts as it
acts much like a new week opening Gap.
- The opening range Gap law is where we close when the bell rings at 4 o'clock New York time.
- This is basically the midpoint or consequent encouragement of the new day opening Gap that started last
evening (my local time).
- Price rallied, took out buy side, then traded back down to the consequent correction of a new week
opening Gap. This is an old new week opening Gap, not the present one.
- The opening range Gap High is the opening price at 9:30 AM. I made a mistake yesterday and had it set
at 9:00 AM because I was busy talking and setting things up.

Next week begins what many call Easter; to me, it's Passover, which I observe. I'm looking to see if we
expand up into that 4104.75 level. Animation on the upside could take us into 4115 to 4119.5. We've been
one-sided all this week since Tuesday, so it'll be interesting to see how they close this trading week going
into a holiday week next week.
- I will not be doing any Twitter, YouTube, or anything with social media; I'll be completely away from all
social media, no emails, no old students' correspondence, nothing.
- I'm resting, celebrating, and observing Passover.
- This morning, we're looking for an opportunity to observe between 10 o'clock and 11 o'clock to see if we
can get a Silver Bullet setup. I'll walk you through it as we enter the 10 o'clock hour. Right now, we're just
waiting for price to mark time until we get into that hour.

section_title: CURRENT MARKET STRUCTURE & SELL SIDE TARGETS
content:
There's a volume imbalance right in here on a four-minute chart. You can see where I'm anchored at the
9:30 opening price, which is the opening range Gap High. You have to be careful when you have price
action like this where we've already pressed up overnight.
- We created a run at the 8:30 news.
- We pushed above it with this high.
- We created another swing high above that one, and now another high above that one.
- You have to be really careful on a Friday where the market's been one-sided. Even though I like 4104 and
three-quarters, we could see some kind of a move lower here that would be attacking some measure of sell
side. Near-term sell side would be right below this lower area.

Looking at the Dollar Index (dxy on TradingView), it looks like we could see a run to 102.25. There's sell
side resting below that, which gives allowance for further upside on ES, indicating a short-term risk-on
scenario. I like how the Dollar Index on the five-minute chart shows a balanced buy-selling efficiency.
- The big five-minute down close candle at 8:30 AM was re-entered at the 9:05 and 9:10 candles.
- We have been heavy since then, trading softer from 102.40.
- Sell side is resting right around 102.25. That's the level I'm watching to see if we get any further animation.
- If it goes below it and then rejects it, that's problematic for continuation on the upside for ES this morning.
- But if we just wash out the 102.25 level and trend towards the 102.15 to 102.10 level, we could see north
of 4104 on ES.
- Again, the focus is the setup that forms between 10 o'clock and 11 o'clock for a five-handle run,
something I teach my students to start with. Begin there; don't try to hit home runs.

This price action, even though it's respecting levels that I teach, has been a little wonky this morning. For
those not familiar with English slang, it's not really pretty, it's not real nice. This entire week has been a little
wonky in my opinion. So if you had a little bit of difficulty this week, it's okay, you're in good company; most
everybody else had the same feeling.
- While we're waiting for the 10 o'clock hour to begin, start hunting our 'third eye gap' setup, which will be a
Silver Bullet.
- You're probably wondering what I'll be doing next week and running bets right now whether I'll do a space
or tweet. I won't, and you will lose that bet. I won't even know what's going on. I'm not even going to look at
YouTube or any social media whatsoever.
- I'll be reading, doing Bible study, with friends and family, and we're traveling, so we won't be connected to
the internet per se. My cell phone will be off in my drawer, so I won't have any kind of enticement to look at
anything social media.
- Now, that doesn't remove the TikTok onslaught that my wife likes to show me, 'Look at this, look at this.'

The Dollar Index is still gravitating towards that 102.25 sell side on the five-minute chart. It has a little bit of
sell side resting below 102.10. So if we animate on the downside for the Dollar Index, that could be a little
bit of an injection of strength to the upside for ES.
- We are looking for ES to get to the 4104.75 level.
- How we trade to it and how we trade once we trade it is what I'm watching.

For those of you wondering where the 4104.75 level comes from:
- Pull up your daily chart for ES (ES M 2023) on TradingView.
- Go to the daily chart of ES on March 6th of 2023.
- There's a 'premium link,' meaning it's the wick that's above the candle. Split that in half, and you'll be able
to see its consequent encouragement.
- Take a moment to find it yourself. Don't be lazy and just wait for me to do it; you have to learn how to do
this yourself.
- There's no indicator that plots this stuff. I do everything old school; I like everything typed out. I don't want
an indicator to plot that stuff for me.

The levels are really written online; this is the secret: it's a notepad. All the levels that I am interested in, all
the ones noted on this chart here, I have them in numerical format, just in the order of importance.
- When I start the day, I write down the opening price in relationship to the hierarchy of all these price levels.
- At some point, once you understand what you're looking for, you really don't need a chart, and you can
just trade off of quotes coming to you. I don't advise you do that if you're new and don't know what you're
doing.
- But when you understand time, you can trade just off of that.

So far, the Dollar Index hasn't really made its way into that 102.25 level just yet. It might be wanting to
rebalance that little area that's created on the most recent five-minute candlestick that's now forming.
- In other words, that little tiny space on the 9:50 five-minute candle on DXY up to the low of the previous
five-minute candle (9:45) with the low of 102.29.
- It would be nice to see that small little gap that's still there remain there, and then animation to the
downside. I'd like to see 102.10 breached and get below 102 for the dollar. Just so you know, I'm not trying
to pick the top on ES.

I think the afternoon session today, from 2:30 PM to 4:00 PM, will probably be very busy. You'll likely see a
lot of nice price swings. Why didn't I live stream that? Because I have a wife that tells me she needs my
attention on her and family at specific times, and she's calling the shots on that.

Okay, watch the dollar digging into that 102.25 sell side now. It's 10:01 AM. It just went underneath the low
on that five-minute candlestick for sell side on DXY. It did not get any kind of expansion below it; it was only
a very small movement below. It's back inside that little gap I mentioned. Watch the 102.29 level on the
dollar.

So far, everything's agreeing on that higher high for the three indices. The Dow has confirmed a higher
high. We're on ES right now on a one-minute chart. What we're looking at is the relationship comparatively
and respectively with these higher highs.
- We'll look at NASDAQ first; you've had higher highs there.
- And higher highs in the Dow.
- We haven't had any kind of indication of waning sponsorship across all three indices, and again, we don't
try to pick tops.

Now, when I'm looking at price like this, and pretty much this entire week really, you see all the give and
take back and forth in here. See how it's back and forth, back and forth, but still gravitating towards levels
that we can anticipate seeing them traded to. But it doesn't give us a real clean area of, 'this is where I
would like to enter,' or 'this is a very low risk, high probability entry.'
- When I see price action like this, I refer to it as being 'spotty.' It's a little too messy; it's not precise; it's not
allowing for precision.
- It may have a hallmark of going to levels that we talk about and what I've been referring to all this week,
but just because it goes to that level doesn't mean that it's offered us (or, speaking in personal terms, it
hasn't offered me) a whole lot of precise entry points this week.
- So when it's like that, you have to be very patient. Don't demand a whole lot because the market's going
to do what it's going to do regardless. If it's not presenting it to you in a manner that is very obvious, where
it's one-sided, you can clearly see an inefficiency, you can clearly see a run-on stops, and then expect it to
go the other direction. I haven't seen much of that this week.
- So, like I said, if you've been studying it, looking at it, or trying to paper trade or whatever, and you've had
adversities that you feel are not common, it's because of what I'm outlining here. It's been a very difficult
week for price delivery; it's real spotty, it's real just messy.

Okay, the dollar is going to need to make its lower lows here; it's going to do it. Otherwise, it wouldn't bang
inside the 102.25 to 102.41 range on the five-minute chart. I don't like this: see how you can draw an
imaginary trend line, diagonal support? I don't like that at all. When my lines look real clean like that, I don't
like that. Meaning that it could easily just spike down here and upset that smoothness that would be
considered diagonal support, which I don't have any faith in.

So I already know that the 10 o'clock to 11 o'clock hour is going to be formidable in terms of finding the
Silver Bullet setup. But I'm committed to spend the entire hour with you and outline it as I see it. Do not
push the button, don't copy it, don't try to go in and do something with your live account, or your funded
account, or your challenge that you want to be in your combine.

section_title: FOREX PAIR ANALYSIS FOR RISK SENTIMENT
content:
I'm looking across Forex pairs to see if I get a better read on risk on or risk off.
- Euro/Dollar has a little bit of buy side on the five-minute chart just above 1.0895. It's gotten very close to
cleaning up that 5 o'clock in the morning short-term high.
- Pound/Dollar is a bit of a mess. I'm sorry, at 1.24 even, there's buy side there. So if we can get that one up
to 1.25 to give up the ghost on dollar, those areas could be traded to. It's not much of a range, admittedly,
but that's what I see on the Forex front.

The Dow looks really (and I'm going to be very careful saying this) but it looks very toppy, looks very like it's
run out of steam on the upside. But you have to be careful on days like this because it can still pump up
there and usually make it a larger extension to the upside where you don't expect a big range move to
come in, and they deliver it. We're kind of just wherever you... All right, the Dollar Index is trying for that
102.25 level to wash that out more prominently. I don't know what the membership level for TradingView is
that you need to have to get two charts side by side. I don't know offhand what that is because I have the
highest form of whatever.
